mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show a positive mean during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack reduces consumer spending growth and corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal income and housing starts signals a synchronized contraction in consumer demand and construction activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a positive mean forward return observed in >60% of co-occurring decline events.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime, persistent CPI growth above 200 erodes real corporate profits and forces aggressive monetary tightening, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >50% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 200, or the predictive IC drops below zero during high inflation sub-samples.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and rising corporate revenues, which directly supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns, with a negative mean return observed in >55% of expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly nonfarm payrolls additions exceeding 200 thousand signal robust labor demand and rising household income, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of months where PAYEMS growth surpasses 200 thousand, or the signal shows no statistical significance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and rising real personal income indicates wealth effects outpacing production, which fuels equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence fails to predict positive returns, with negative forward returns occurring in >55% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below 110, manufacturing weakness triggers supply chain bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive in >50% of periods where INDPRO drops below 110, or the predictive accuracy falls below random chance in out-of-sample validation.
